intent: Check whether the candidate understands the practical difference between any and unknown and can explain how to keep type safety when handling uncertain data.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between any and unknown in TypeScript, and when would you choose unknown over any?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that any disables useful type checking for that value while unknown requires narrowing before use.
Uses unknown for external or uncertain values such as API responses, catch errors, localStorage, or message payloads.
Mentions narrowing through typeof, instanceof, property checks, schema parsing, or user-defined type guards.
Understands that type assertions can bypass safety and should be localized and justified.

redFlags: Says any and unknown are basically the same.
Uses as SomeType as the default way to handle external data.
Cannot explain how to safely read a property from an unknown value.

scoringHints.description: Clearly contrasts any and unknown, gives realistic boundary examples, and explains narrowing before use.
Understands unknown requires checks but misses one nuance such as unsafe assertions or catch error handling.
Can repeat the rule but cannot apply it to real data flow.
Answer is too vague to confirm practical TypeScript safety.

followUps.question: If JSON.parse returns an unknown value, what exact checks would you do before using user.email?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete narrowing and boundary reasoning.

followUps.question: What risk do you introduce by writing value as User without validation?

en.title: any versus unknown
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between any and unknown in TypeScript, and when would you choose unknown over any?
en.displayIntent: Check TypeScript safety around uncertain values.
guidance: Use an example involving external or untrusted data.
Explain the narrowing step before accessing properties.
commonMistakes: Treating unknown as a more verbose any.
Using type assertions instead of validation.

vi.title: any và unknown
vi.displayQuestion: any và unknown khác nhau thế nào trong TypeScript, và khi nào bạn chọn unknown thay vì any?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng giữ type safety khi xử lý giá trị chưa chắc chắn.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ liên quan tới dữ liệu bên ngoài hoặc không đáng tin.
Giải thích bước narrow trước khi đọc property.
commonMistakes: Xem unknown chỉ là any dài dòng hơn.
Dùng type assertion thay cho validation.

ja.title: any と unknown
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript の any と unknown は何が違い、どのような場面で any ではなく unknown を選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: 不確かな値を扱うときの TypeScript の安全性を確認する。
guidance: 外部データや信頼できないデータの例を使う。
property にアクセスする前の narrowing を説明する。
commonMistakes: unknown を冗長な any として扱う。
validation の代わりに type assertion を使う。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Narrowing
sourceReferences.note: Used for narrowing and type guard concepts.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript interview question coverage
sourceReferences.note: Used for interview topic coverage; probe wording is original.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can choose between type aliases and interfaces based on extension, declaration merging, unions, and readability.
primaryQuestion: How do type aliases and interfaces differ in TypeScript, and how would you decide which one to use for a domain model?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that both can describe object shapes for many common cases.
Mentions interface extension and declaration merging as useful for public object contracts or augmentation.
Mentions type aliases for unions, intersections, primitives, tuples, mapped or conditional compositions.
Chooses consistently based on project convention, API surface, and expected evolution.

redFlags: Claims one is always strictly better for every use case.
Cannot explain declaration merging or why it can be useful or risky.
Confuses runtime classes with compile-time object shape declarations.

scoringHints.description: Gives a nuanced comparison with examples and connects the choice to API evolution and team convention.
Knows the main differences but misses one dimension such as union aliases or declaration merging risk.
Has memorized preferences but cannot justify them technically.
Answer does not show reliable object modeling knowledge.

followUps.question: When would declaration merging be helpful, and when could it surprise a team?
How would you represent a result type like Success | Failure?
followUps.purpose: Check whether candidate knows where type aliases fit better.

en.title: type aliases and interfaces
en.displayQuestion: How do type aliases and interfaces differ in TypeScript, and how would you decide which one to use for a domain model?
en.displayIntent: Check practical TypeScript modeling choices.
guidance: Compare extension, merging, and union modeling.
Tie the answer to a team convention or public API.
commonMistakes: Saying type and interface are identical in every way.
Ignoring declaration merging.

vi.title: type alias và interface
vi.displayQuestion: type alias và interface khác nhau thế nào trong TypeScript, và bạn quyết định dùng cái nào cho domain model ra sao?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra lựa chọn modeling thực tế trong TypeScript.
guidance: So sánh extension, merging và cách model union.
Gắn câu trả lời với convention của team hoặc public API.
commonMistakes: Nói type và interface giống nhau hoàn toàn.
Bỏ qua declaration merging.

ja.title: type alias と interface
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript の type alias と interface は何が違い、domain model ではどちらを使うかをどう判断しますか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript の実践的な modeling 判断を確認する。
guidance: extension、merging、union modeling を比較する。
team convention や public API と結びつける。
commonMistakes: type と interface が完全に同じだと言う。
declaration merging を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Everyday Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for type alias and interface fundamentals.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Declaration Merging
sourceReferences.note: Used for interface merging behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands union types, control-flow narrowing, and why TypeScript only allows access to fields common to all union members before narrowing.
primaryQuestion: If a value has type User | Admin, why can TypeScript block access to adminOnlyField, and how would you narrow the value safely?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that a union value may be any member of the union at runtime.
States that only fields common to all members are safe before narrowing.
Uses checks such as discriminant fields, in operator, typeof, instanceof, or custom type predicates.
Avoids unsafe casts unless there is an external invariant and it is documented.

redFlags: Forces access with as Admin without proving the branch.
Cannot explain why a common property is allowed but a member-specific property is not.
Thinks TypeScript performs runtime validation automatically.

scoringHints.description: Explains union safety and shows a concrete narrowing strategy with correct runtime checks.
Understands narrowing but misses one safe pattern or overuses assertions.
Knows union syntax but struggles with property access reasoning.
Answer does not demonstrate TypeScript control-flow analysis understanding.

followUps.question: Can you write a small branch that checks whether account is an Admin before reading permissions?
followUps.purpose: Turn the concept into concrete code reasoning.

followUps.question: What could go wrong if the API sends a User but your code casts it to Admin?

en.title: Union narrowing
en.displayQuestion: If a value has type User | Admin, why can TypeScript block access to adminOnlyField, and how would you narrow the value safely?
en.displayIntent: Check union type and narrowing fundamentals.
guidance: Use a concrete User/Admin example.
Explain why TypeScript needs runtime evidence.
commonMistakes: Using assertions before proving the variant.
Assuming TypeScript validates runtime payloads.

vi.title: Narrow union type
vi.displayQuestion: Nếu một giá trị có type User | Admin, vì sao TypeScript có thể chặn việc đọc adminOnlyField, và bạn narrow giá trị đó an toàn thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng union type và narrowing.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ User/Admin cụ thể.
Giải thích vì sao TypeScript cần bằng chứng ở runtime.
commonMistakes: Dùng assertion trước khi chứng minh variant.
Giả định TypeScript validate payload runtime.

ja.title: union type の narrowing
ja.displayQuestion: 値の型が User | Admin のとき、なぜ TypeScript は adminOnlyField へのアクセスを止めることがあり、どう安全に narrow しますか。
ja.displayIntent: union type と narrowing の基礎を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な User/Admin の例を使う。
TypeScript が runtime の根拠を必要とする理由を説明する。
commonMistakes: variant を証明する前に assertion を使う。
TypeScript が runtime payload を検証すると仮定する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Narrowing
sourceReferences.note: Used for control-flow narrowing and type guard patterns.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can reason about optional properties, nullability, strictNullChecks, optional chaining, and defaulting without hiding bugs.
primaryQuestion: How do optional properties, undefined, null, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing work together in TypeScript?

expectedSignals.label: Distinguishes a missing property from a value explicitly set to undefined or null.
Explains that strictNullChecks forces handling of possibly null or undefined values.
Uses optional chaining for safe access and nullish coalescing for defaults that should not treat empty string or zero as missing.
Mentions exactOptionalPropertyTypes when discussing precise optional property semantics.

redFlags: Uses non-null assertion everywhere to silence errors.
Confuses || defaults with ?? defaults and breaks valid falsy values.
Cannot explain how strictNullChecks changes code behavior.

scoringHints.description: Connects optional properties, nullish values, compiler settings, and concrete safe-access code.
Understands optional chaining and defaults but misses exact optional semantics or null versus undefined.
Can use syntax but cannot explain correctness risks.
Answer does not show nullability reasoning.

followUps.question: What is the difference between count || 10 and count ?? 10 when count can be 0?
When is user!.email acceptable, and what evidence should exist before using it?

en.title: Optional and nullish values
en.displayQuestion: How do optional properties, undefined, null, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing work together in TypeScript?
en.displayIntent: Check practical nullability handling.
guidance: Use a payload or config object example.
Mention compiler settings when relevant.
commonMistakes: Replacing checks with non-null assertions.
Using || where ?? is required.

vi.title: Optional và giá trị nullish
vi.displayQuestion: Optional property, undefined, null, optional chaining và nullish coalescing phối hợp với nhau thế nào trong TypeScript?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách xử lý nullability trong thực tế.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ payload hoặc config object.
Nhắc tới compiler setting khi phù hợp.
commonMistakes: Thay check bằng non-null assertion.
Dùng || trong trường hợp cần ??.

ja.title: optional と nullish value
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript で optional property、undefined、null、optional chaining、nullish coalescing はどのように関係しますか。
ja.displayIntent: 実践的な nullability handling を確認する。
guidance: payload や config object の例を使う。
必要なら compiler setting に触れる。
commonMistakes: check を non-null assertion で置き換える。
?? が必要な場面で || を使う。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript TSConfig: strict
sourceReferences.note: Used for strict type checking context.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript TSConfig: exactOptionalPropertyTypes
sourceReferences.note: Used for optional property semantics.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can debug TypeScript compiler errors by following the data shape and the assignment site instead of suppressing the error.
primaryQuestion: A TypeScript error says Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'. How would you debug and fix it?

expectedSignals.label: Finds where the value becomes optional or possibly undefined.
Checks whether the receiving function truly requires a string or should accept missing values.
Chooses an appropriate fix such as validation, defaulting, early return, type refinement, or API contract change.
Avoids blindly using as string or non-null assertion as the first fix.

redFlags: Immediately adds ! or as string without checking the source.
Changes the target type to any to unblock compilation.
Cannot explain the runtime case TypeScript is warning about.

scoringHints.description: Systematically traces the type, explains the real missing-value case, and chooses a fix that matches the domain contract.
Fixes the error safely but does not fully discuss whether the API contract should change.
Recognizes undefined but relies on surface syntax without domain reasoning.
Answer does not show compiler-error debugging skill.

followUps.question: What would you inspect first: the variable declaration, the caller, or the callee contract, and why?
What bug can user.name! hide if the API sometimes omits name?

en.title: Debug string or undefined
en.displayQuestion: A TypeScript error says Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'. How would you debug and fix it?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate practical TypeScript compiler-error debugging.
guidance: Trace the value from source to assignment.
Choose a fix based on the domain contract.
commonMistakes: Silencing the compiler with ! or as string.
Changing useful types to any.

vi.title: Debug string hoặc undefined
vi.displayQuestion: Một lỗi TypeScript báo Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'. Bạn debug và sửa thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug lỗi compiler TypeScript thực tế.
guidance: Lần theo giá trị từ nguồn tới nơi assign.
Chọn cách fix dựa trên contract của domain.
commonMistakes: Tắt lỗi compiler bằng ! hoặc as string.
Đổi type hữu ích thành any.

ja.title: string | undefined の debug
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript が Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string' と出した場合、どう debug して修正しますか。
ja.displayIntent: 実践的な TypeScript compiler error の debug 力を評価する。
guidance: source から assignment まで値を追跡する。
domain contract に基づいて修正を選ぶ。
commonMistakes: ! や as string で compiler を黙らせる。
有用な型を any に変える。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript TSConfig: strict
sourceReferences.note: Used for strict nullability and type checking context.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Narrowing
sourceReferences.note: Used for safe refinement patterns.

intent: Check whether the candidate can model finite states with literal unions or enums and understand the runtime implications of each choice.
primaryQuestion: When would you model a finite set of values with a string literal union instead of an enum in TypeScript?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that string literal unions are type-level and usually emit no runtime object.
Mentions enums can provide a runtime object but add emitted JavaScript and have interop considerations.
Uses literal unions for simple API statuses, component variants, or discriminants.
Considers readability, refactoring, external contract compatibility, and project conventions.

redFlags: Thinks every finite value set must be an enum.
Cannot explain that TypeScript types are erased but enums can exist at runtime.
Ignores external API string values when designing the type.

scoringHints.description: Compares literal unions and enums through runtime output, API compatibility, and maintainability.
Chooses literal unions appropriately but misses enum runtime implications.
Understands the syntax but not the trade-off.
Answer does not show finite-state modeling judgment.

followUps.question: What does an enum give you that a literal union does not?
How would you type a REST API status field that returns 'draft', 'active', or 'archived'?

en.title: Literal unions versus enums
en.displayQuestion: When would you model a finite set of values with a string literal union instead of an enum in TypeScript?
en.displayIntent: Check finite-value modeling and runtime trade-offs.
guidance: Discuss emitted JavaScript and API compatibility.
Use a status or variant example.
commonMistakes: Defaulting to enum for every finite set.
Ignoring type erasure and runtime output.

vi.title: Literal union và enum
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn model một tập giá trị hữu hạn bằng string literal union thay vì enum trong TypeScript?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách model finite value và trade-off runtime.
guidance: Nói về JavaScript được emit và compatibility với API.
Dùng ví dụ status hoặc variant.
commonMistakes: Luôn dùng enum cho mọi tập giá trị hữu hạn.
Bỏ qua type erasure và runtime output.

ja.title: literal union と enum
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript で有限個の値を表すとき、どのような場合に enum ではなく string literal union を使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: finite value modeling と runtime trade-off を確認する。
guidance: emit される JavaScript と API compatibility を話す。
status や variant の例を使う。
commonMistakes: 有限集合に常に enum を使う。
type erasure と runtime output を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Literal Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for literal type modeling.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Enums
sourceReferences.note: Used for enum behavior and trade-offs.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design generic functions that preserve relationships between inputs and outputs without falling back to any.
primaryQuestion: How would you design a generic helper that returns one property from an object while ensuring the key and return type stay linked?

expectedSignals.label: Uses generics with a key constraint such as K extends keyof T.
Explains that the return type should be T[K] instead of a broad union or any.
Understands why unconstrained T cannot be indexed safely.
Can discuss type inference and when explicit type parameters are or are not needed.

redFlags: Uses any for the object or key and loses type information.
Cannot explain why key must be constrained to keyof T.
Returns unknown or T[keyof T] without preserving the selected key relationship.

scoringHints.description: Designs a precise generic signature, explains constraints and indexed access types, and shows how inference behaves.
Gets the K extends keyof T pattern but misses one nuance such as preserving T[K].
Knows generics syntax but cannot model the key/value relationship.
Answer does not demonstrate reusable generic API design.

followUps.question: What should getProperty(user, 'email') return if user.email is string | undefined?
Why is K extends keyof T safer than key: string?

en.title: Generic key constraints
en.displayQuestion: How would you design a generic helper that returns one property from an object while ensuring the key and return type stay linked?
en.displayIntent: Check generics, keyof, and indexed access reasoning.
guidance: Discuss the function signature, not only the implementation.
Explain how inference preserves the selected property type.
commonMistakes: Using any for the object or key.
Returning a broad union instead of T[K].

vi.title: Generic key constraint
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn thiết kế một generic helper trả về một property của object thế nào để key và return type vẫn liên kết đúng với nhau?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra generics, keyof và indexed access.
guidance: Nói về function signature, không chỉ implementation.
Giải thích inference giữ type của property được chọn thế nào.
commonMistakes: Dùng any cho object hoặc key.
Trả về union quá rộng thay vì T[K].

ja.title: generic key constraint
ja.displayQuestion: object から 1 つの property を返す generic helper を、key と return type の関係を保ったままどう設計しますか。
ja.displayIntent: generics、keyof、indexed access の理解を確認する。
guidance: implementation だけでなく function signature を話す。
選択された property type を inference がどう保つか説明する。
commonMistakes: object や key に any を使う。
T[K] ではなく広すぎる union を返す。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Generics
sourceReferences.note: Used for generic constraints and reusable API concepts.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: keyof Type Operator
sourceReferences.note: Used for keyof and property key constraints.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can model state machines or API result shapes with discriminated unions and exhaustive handling.
primaryQuestion: How would you model loading, success, and error states with a discriminated union, and how would you make missing cases visible?

expectedSignals.label: Uses a shared discriminant field such as status, kind, or state with literal values.
Keeps variant-specific data on the correct branch, such as data only on success and error only on failure.
Explains switch-based narrowing and access to branch-specific fields.
Mentions exhaustive checking with never, strict return types, or lint/compiler support.

redFlags: Models state as multiple optional fields that can create impossible combinations.
Does not understand how the discriminant narrows the union.
Ignores what happens when a new variant is added.

scoringHints.description: Models variants cleanly, explains narrowing, prevents impossible states, and includes exhaustive handling.
Uses discriminated unions correctly but misses exhaustive checking details.
Knows union syntax but leaves ambiguous optional fields.
Answer does not show robust state modeling.

followUps.question: What impossible state can happen if you use { loading?: boolean; data?: Data; error?: Error }?
How would the compiler help you after adding a 'from_cache' state?

en.title: Discriminated union state
en.displayQuestion: How would you model loading, success, and error states with a discriminated union, and how would you make missing cases visible?
en.displayIntent: Check state modeling and exhaustiveness in TypeScript.
guidance: Use a concrete async request state.
Explain how new variants are caught during maintenance.
commonMistakes: Using optional fields that allow impossible states.
Skipping exhaustive checks.

vi.title: Discriminated union cho state
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn model loading, success và error state bằng discriminated union thế nào, và làm sao để case bị thiếu được lộ ra?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách model state và exhaustiveness trong TypeScript.
guidance: Dùng một async request state cụ thể.
Giải thích compiler hỗ trợ khi thêm variant mới.
commonMistakes: Dùng optional field tạo ra impossible state.
Bỏ qua exhaustive check.

ja.title: discriminated union の state
ja.displayQuestion: loading、success、error の状態を discriminated union でどう model し、case 漏れをどう見えるようにしますか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript の state modeling と exhaustiveness を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な async request state を使う。
新しい variant を追加した時に compiler がどう助けるか説明する。
commonMistakes: impossible state を許す optional field を使う。
exhaustive check を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Narrowing
sourceReferences.note: Used for discriminated union and narrowing behavior.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Unions and Intersections
sourceReferences.note: Used for union exhaustiveness concepts.

intent: Check whether the candidate can use built-in utility types to derive related shapes while preserving intent and avoiding accidental loose contracts.
primaryQuestion: How would you use Partial, Pick, Omit, Record, ReturnType, or Awaited to avoid duplicating TypeScript types?

expectedSignals.label: Explains utility types as transformations of existing types.
Chooses specific utilities for practical examples such as update DTOs, public view models, lookup maps, inferred function returns, or promise unwrapping.
Mentions risks such as overusing Partial and making required domain fields accidentally optional.
Understands that derived types should still express clear API boundaries.

redFlags: Uses Partial for every input type without thinking about required fields.
Duplicates similar interfaces manually and lets them drift.
Cannot explain what a utility type is transforming.

scoringHints.description: Uses several utilities accurately, explains why each fits, and discusses over-broad derived type risks.
Knows common utilities but misses boundary and maintenance trade-offs.
Recognizes utility names but cannot apply them to realistic model evolution.
Answer does not show type derivation understanding.

followUps.question: When is Partial<User> too loose for an update request?
How would you type a map from feature flag name to boolean using Record?

en.title: Utility type derivation
en.displayQuestion: How would you use Partial, Pick, Omit, Record, ReturnType, or Awaited to avoid duplicating TypeScript types?
en.displayIntent: Check practical use of TypeScript utility types.
guidance: Use concrete DTO, view model, or helper examples.
Discuss when derived types become too broad.
commonMistakes: Using Partial as a universal update model.
Duplicating interfaces that should be derived.

vi.title: Dẫn xuất type bằng utility type
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn dùng Partial, Pick, Omit, Record, ReturnType hoặc Awaited thế nào để tránh lặp type trong TypeScript?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách dùng utility type trong thực tế.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ DTO, view model hoặc helper cụ thể.
Nói về lúc derived type trở nên quá rộng.
commonMistakes: Dùng Partial như update model cho mọi nơi.
Lặp interface đáng ra nên derive.

ja.title: utility type による derivation
ja.displayQuestion: Partial、Pick、Omit、Record、ReturnType、Awaited を使って TypeScript の型重複をどう避けますか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript utility type の実践的な利用を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な DTO、view model、helper の例を使う。
derived type が広すぎる場合を話す。
commonMistakes: Partial を万能 update model として使う。
derive すべき interface を重複させる。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Utility Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for built-in utility type coverage.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands mapped types, keyof, modifiers, and how to derive systematic object transformations.
primaryQuestion: What are mapped types in TypeScript, and how would you use them to transform every property of a type?

expectedSignals.label: Explains mapped types as iterating over keys of a type using keyof.
Uses examples such as readonly, optional, nullable, validation error maps, or form state maps.
Mentions mapping modifiers like readonly, ?, -readonly, or -?.
Understands when mapped types improve consistency versus when explicit types are clearer.

redFlags: Cannot connect mapped types to keyof.
Uses hand-written duplicate types for mechanical transformations.
Creates overly clever types that obscure a simple domain shape.

scoringHints.description: Explains key iteration, modifiers, realistic transformations, and readability trade-offs.
Understands mapped type basics but misses modifiers or practical limits.
Can name mapped types but cannot design one.
Answer lacks advanced type transformation reasoning.

followUps.question: How would you create a type where every field of User maps to a validation error string array?
When would a mapped type be less readable than writing the type explicitly?

en.title: Mapped type transformations
en.displayQuestion: What are mapped types in TypeScript, and how would you use them to transform every property of a type?
en.displayIntent: Check mapped type and keyof reasoning.
guidance: Use a form, DTO, or validation-map example.
Mention property modifiers if relevant.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that mapped types iterate over keys.
Making type logic too clever for the domain.

vi.title: Biến đổi bằng mapped type
vi.displayQuestion: Mapped type trong TypeScript là gì, và bạn dùng nó thế nào để transform mọi property của một type?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra mapped type và keyof.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ form, DTO hoặc validation map.
Nhắc tới property modifier nếu phù hợp.
commonMistakes: Quên mapped type duyệt qua key.
Làm type logic quá phức tạp so với domain.

ja.title: mapped type transformation
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript の mapped type とは何で、型の全 property を変換するためにどう使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: mapped type と keyof の理解を確認する。
guidance: form、DTO、validation map の例を使う。
必要なら property modifier に触れる。
commonMistakes: mapped type が key を反復することを忘れる。
domain に対して type logic を複雑にしすぎる。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Mapped Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for mapped types and modifiers.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Creating Types from Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for type transformation context.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can explain conditional types, infer, and distributive behavior over unions without treating them as magic.
primaryQuestion: How do conditional types work in TypeScript, and what does it mean that they can distribute over unions?

expectedSignals.label: Explains the SomeType extends OtherType ? True : False shape.
Uses a practical example such as extracting Promise payloads, array elements, or function return types.
Mentions infer for capturing a type from a matched structure.
Explains distributive conditional types and how wrapping in tuples can prevent distribution.

redFlags: Cannot distinguish runtime if statements from type-level conditional types.
Does not understand why a union input can become a union of transformed outputs.
Uses complex conditional types without explaining maintainability costs.

scoringHints.description: Explains conditionals, infer, distribution, non-distribution, and a maintainable real use case.
Understands conditional type basics but misses infer or distributivity control.
Recognizes syntax but cannot reason through union behavior.
Answer does not show advanced type-system understanding.

followUps.question: What would ToArray<string | number> produce if ToArray<T> is T extends any ? T[] : never?
When would you avoid a clever conditional type and write an explicit type instead?

en.title: Conditional type distribution
en.displayQuestion: How do conditional types work in TypeScript, and what does it mean that they can distribute over unions?
en.displayIntent: Check conditional types, infer, and union distribution.
guidance: Walk through a small type transformation.
Explain distribution with a union input.
commonMistakes: Treating conditional types as runtime branches.
Ignoring distributive behavior over unions.

vi.title: Conditional type và distribution
vi.displayQuestion: Conditional type trong TypeScript hoạt động thế nào, và việc chúng có thể distribute over union nghĩa là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra conditional type, infer và union distribution.
guidance: Đi qua một type transformation nhỏ.
Giải thích distribution với union input.
commonMistakes: Xem conditional type như nhánh runtime.
Bỏ qua behavior distribute trên union.

ja.title: conditional type distribution
ja.displayQuestion: TypeScript の conditional type はどう動作し、union に対して distribute するとはどういう意味ですか。
ja.displayIntent: conditional type、infer、union distribution を確認する。
guidance: 小さな type transformation を順に説明する。
union input で distribution を説明する。
commonMistakes: conditional type を runtime branch として扱う。
union での distributive behavior を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Conditional Types
sourceReferences.note: Used for conditional types, infer, and distributivity.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands that TypeScript types are erased at runtime and can place validation at trust boundaries.
primaryQuestion: Why are TypeScript types not enough when reading data from an API, localStorage, a queue, or user input, and where would you validate?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that TypeScript checks static code but does not validate external data at runtime.
Identifies trust boundaries such as HTTP responses, request bodies, localStorage, message queues, and environment variables.
Uses parsing or schema validation to turn unknown data into validated domain types.
Discusses error handling, normalization, logging, and backward compatibility for malformed payloads.

redFlags: Says TypeScript prevents bad API data from reaching runtime.
Casts external payloads directly to domain types.
Validates only deep inside business logic instead of at clear boundaries.

scoringHints.description: Defines trust boundaries, explains type erasure, proposes validation/parsing, and covers failure handling.
Understands runtime validation but misses compatibility or normalization concerns.
Knows validation is useful but cannot say where it belongs or why.
Answer lacks static-versus-runtime type reasoning.

followUps.question: Where would you validate a user object loaded from localStorage before putting it into application state?
What happens to TypeScript interfaces after compilation?

en.title: Runtime validation boundaries
en.displayQuestion: Why are TypeScript types not enough when reading data from an API, localStorage, a queue, or user input, and where would you validate?
en.displayIntent: Check runtime validation and trust-boundary reasoning.
guidance: Name concrete trust boundaries.
Explain parsing unknown data into a safe domain type.
commonMistakes: Trusting TypeScript for runtime payloads.
Casting unknown data directly to domain types.

vi.title: Boundary runtime validation
vi.displayQuestion: Vì sao TypeScript type không đủ khi đọc data từ API, localStorage, queue hoặc user input, và bạn validate ở đâu?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra runtime validation và trust boundary.
guidance: Nêu trust boundary cụ thể.
Giải thích parse unknown data thành domain type an toàn.
commonMistakes: Tin TypeScript cho payload runtime.
Cast unknown data thẳng sang domain type.

ja.title: runtime validation boundary
ja.displayQuestion: API、localStorage、queue、user input から data を読むとき、なぜ TypeScript の型だけでは不十分で、どこで validate しますか。
ja.displayIntent: runtime validation と trust boundary reasoning を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な trust boundary を挙げる。
unknown data を安全な domain type に parse することを説明する。
commonMistakes: runtime payload に TypeScript だけを信頼する。
unknown data を直接 domain type に cast する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for runtime checks and narrowing patterns.

sourceReferences.note: Used for interview topic coverage; probe wording is original.

intent: Evaluate practical debugging of generic inference issues where TypeScript infers a type that is too broad, too narrow, or polluted by literals and unions.
primaryQuestion: A generic function infers the wrong type and downstream code becomes too broad or too narrow. How would you debug the signature?

expectedSignals.label: Inspects the function signature, type parameter positions, constraints, and return type.
Checks whether inference is coming from the right argument or being widened by arrays, literals, or unions.
Uses techniques such as overloads, explicit type arguments, const assertions, satisfies, helper parameters, or separating input/output generics when appropriate.
Avoids fixing inference by replacing the generic with any.

redFlags: Changes generic parameters to any to make the error disappear.
Does not inspect how the type parameter is inferred.
Cannot distinguish inference problems from runtime bugs.

scoringHints.description: Systematically debugs inference sources and chooses a precise signature or call-site adjustment.
Understands inference direction but misses one tool such as satisfies or overloads.
Recognizes a generic problem but fixes it with broad casts.
Answer does not show generic debugging skill.

followUps.question: How would you inspect the inferred type at the call site without changing runtime code?
When would you use an overload instead of a conditional return type?

en.title: Debug generic inference
en.displayQuestion: A generic function infers the wrong type and downstream code becomes too broad or too narrow. How would you debug the signature?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate debugging of TypeScript generic inference.
guidance: Discuss type parameter positions and inference sources.
Include one fix that preserves type safety.
commonMistakes: Replacing the generic with any.
Ignoring where inference comes from.

vi.title: Debug generic inference
vi.displayQuestion: Một generic function infer sai type khiến code phía sau quá rộng hoặc quá hẹp. Bạn debug signature thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug generic inference TypeScript.
guidance: Nói về vị trí type parameter và nguồn inference.
Đưa một cách fix vẫn giữ type safety.
commonMistakes: Thay generic bằng any.
Bỏ qua nguồn inference.

ja.title: generic inference の debug
ja.displayQuestion: generic function が誤った型を infer し、後続コードが広すぎる、または狭すぎる型になります。signature をどう debug しますか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript generic inference の debug 力を評価する。
guidance: type parameter の位置と inference source を話す。
type safety を保つ修正を 1 つ含める。
commonMistakes: generic を any に置き換える。
inference がどこから来るかを無視する。

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Generics
sourceReferences.note: Used for generic inference and constraints.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript Handbook: Type Inference

intent: Assess judgment around TypeScript strictness settings, migration cost, and risk reduction in an existing codebase.
primaryQuestion: A team wants to turn on strict TypeScript settings in an existing project. What would you evaluate, and how would you roll it out?

expectedSignals.label: Explains the value of strict checks for nullability, implicit any, this binding, function types, and related correctness risks.
Assesses current error volume, ownership, test coverage, CI impact, and release risk.
Plans incremental rollout by package, directory, tsconfig layering, or selected flags.
Avoids mass suppression and tracks remaining debt with clear owners.

redFlags: Turns on strict everywhere without measuring the blast radius.
Adds broad ts-ignore comments or any types to pass CI.
Rejects strictness only because it creates many errors, without triage.

scoringHints.description: Balances safety benefit, migration cost, rollout plan, CI policy, and debt tracking.
Has an incremental plan but misses ownership or test coverage.
Understands strict is useful but lacks rollout strategy.
Answer lacks TypeScript migration judgment.

followUps.question: What baseline would you collect before enabling strict mode?
Which strict flag might you enable first and why?

en.title: Strict TypeScript rollout
en.displayQuestion: A team wants to turn on strict TypeScript settings in an existing project. What would you evaluate, and how would you roll it out?
en.displayIntent: Assess TypeScript strictness migration judgment.
guidance: Discuss risk, error volume, CI, and ownership.
Prefer an incremental migration plan.
commonMistakes: Turning on strict without measuring impact.
Mass-suppressing errors to pass CI.

vi.title: Rollout TypeScript strict
vi.displayQuestion: Một team muốn bật strict TypeScript settings trong project hiện có. Bạn đánh giá gì và rollout thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá judgment khi migration TypeScript strict.
guidance: Nói về risk, số lỗi, CI và ownership.
Ưu tiên migration từng bước.
commonMistakes: Bật strict mà không đo impact.
Suppress lỗi hàng loạt để pass CI.

ja.title: TypeScript strict rollout
ja.displayQuestion: 既存 project で strict TypeScript settings を有効にしたい場合、何を評価し、どう rollout しますか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript strict migration の判断力を評価する。
guidance: risk、error volume、CI、ownership を話す。
段階的な migration plan を優先する。
commonMistakes: impact を測らず strict を有効にする。
CI を通すために error を大量 suppress する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for strict mode behavior and migration topics.

sourceReferences.label: TypeScript TSConfig: noUncheckedIndexedAccess

intent: Evaluate senior ability to design TypeScript API surfaces that are precise, evolvable, and not overly complex.
primaryQuestion: How would you design TypeScript types for a public SDK or shared package so consumers get strong inference without a fragile or overly clever API?

expectedSignals.label: Designs stable exported types and avoids leaking internal implementation details.
Uses generics, overloads, discriminated unions, or conditional types only where they improve consumer ergonomics.
Considers semantic versioning, backwards compatibility, declaration output, documentation, and examples.
Tests type behavior with compile-time assertions or consumer-style examples.

redFlags: Optimizes for clever type tricks instead of clear consumer experience.
Breaks public types casually without migration guidance.
Cannot explain how exported declarations will be consumed outside the package.

scoringHints.description: Balances inference, readability, exported API stability, type tests, and versioning.
Good type design instincts but misses declaration output or type-test validation.
Can write advanced types but does not reason about public API maintenance.
Answer lacks senior-level TypeScript API design judgment.

followUps.question: How would you verify that a type change did not break downstream consumers?
followUps.purpose: Check type testing and compatibility validation.

followUps.question: When would you choose overloads instead of a generic conditional return type for an SDK method?

en.title: Public TypeScript API design
en.displayQuestion: How would you design TypeScript types for a public SDK or shared package so consumers get strong inference without a fragile or overly clever API?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate senior TypeScript API design judgment.
guidance: Discuss consumer ergonomics and exported type stability.
Include type tests or declaration validation.
commonMistakes: Overusing clever conditional types.
Ignoring semantic versioning of public types.

vi.title: Thiết kế public TypeScript API
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn thiết kế TypeScript type cho public SDK hoặc shared package thế nào để consumer có inference tốt mà API không fragile hoặc quá clever?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá judgment thiết kế TypeScript API ở mức senior.
guidance: Nói về consumer ergonomics và độ ổn định của exported type.
Bao gồm type test hoặc validation declaration.
commonMistakes: Lạm dụng conditional type quá clever.
Bỏ qua semantic versioning của public type.

ja.title: public TypeScript API design
ja.displayQuestion: public SDK や shared package の TypeScript 型を、consumer が強い inference を得つつ fragile でも clever すぎもしない API にするにはどう設計しますか。
ja.displayIntent: senior level の TypeScript API design 判断を評価する。
guidance: consumer ergonomics と exported type stability を話す。
type test や declaration validation を含める。
commonMistakes: clever な conditional type を過度に使う。
public type の semantic versioning を無視する。

intent: Assess senior judgment around when advanced type modeling improves safety and when it harms readability, build performance, or team velocity.
primaryQuestion: How do you decide whether a complex TypeScript type is worth it, versus using simpler types plus runtime checks or tests?

expectedSignals.label: Evaluates risk, frequency of mistakes, API surface size, team familiarity, and maintenance cost.
Considers compiler performance, editor responsiveness, error readability, and onboarding impact.
Uses advanced types when they encode meaningful invariants and prevent real bugs.
Chooses runtime validation or tests for value-level constraints that TypeScript cannot prove.

redFlags: Assumes more advanced types are always better.
Rejects advanced types entirely without considering safety benefits.
Ignores developer experience and error-message readability.

scoringHints.description: Balances safety, maintainability, runtime validation, DX, and team capability with concrete examples.
Good trade-off reasoning but misses compiler/editor performance or onboarding.
Takes a one-sided position without evaluating context.
Answer lacks senior-level trade-off analysis.

followUps.question: Give an example of an invariant you would encode in types and one you would leave to runtime validation.
followUps.purpose: Force concrete boundary between type-level and runtime-level checks.

followUps.question: What signal would tell you a type abstraction is hurting developer experience?
followUps.purpose: Check operational and team impact awareness.

en.title: Type safety versus complexity
en.displayQuestion: How do you decide whether a complex TypeScript type is worth it, versus using simpler types plus runtime checks or tests?
en.displayIntent: Assess senior trade-off judgment for type-system complexity.
guidance: Discuss invariants, DX, compiler performance, and team maturity.
Separate compile-time and runtime guarantees.
commonMistakes: Treating clever types as automatically better.
Ignoring runtime validation needs.

vi.title: Type safety và complexity
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn quyết định một TypeScript type phức tạp có đáng dùng không, so với type đơn giản hơn cộng runtime check hoặc test, như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá trade-off senior về độ phức tạp của type system.
guidance: Nói về invariant, DX, compiler performance và maturity của team.
Tách compile-time guarantee và runtime guarantee.
commonMistakes: Xem type clever là luôn tốt hơn.
Bỏ qua nhu cầu runtime validation.

ja.title: type safety と complexity
ja.displayQuestion: 複雑な TypeScript 型を使う価値があるか、より単純な型と runtime check/test を使うべきかをどう判断しますか。
ja.displayIntent: type-system complexity に関する senior trade-off 判断を評価する。
guidance: invariant、DX、compiler performance、team maturity を話す。
compile-time guarantee と runtime guarantee を分ける。
commonMistakes: clever な型を常に良いものとして扱う。
runtime validation の必要性を無視する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for advanced type composition topics.

sourceReferences.note: Used for advanced type-system trade-off context.

intent: Evaluate senior debugging of TypeScript build and module resolution problems in a multi-package codebase.
primaryQuestion: A monorepo TypeScript build fails only in CI with stale declarations or module resolution errors. How would you diagnose it?

expectedSignals.label: Checks tsconfig inheritance, paths, moduleResolution, outDir/rootDir, declaration output, and project references.
Compares local and CI TypeScript versions, package manager lockfile, build cache, and clean build behavior.
Inspects dependency graph order and whether referenced packages emit declarations before consumers build.
Uses traceResolution, build verbose output, cache invalidation, and minimal reproduction where useful.

redFlags: Only reruns CI without collecting evidence.
Deletes build caches permanently without understanding the invalidation issue.
Ignores version skew or tsconfig differences between packages.

scoringHints.description: Uses systematic CI/local comparison, TypeScript resolution tools, project graph reasoning, and durable cache/build fixes.
Good build-debug flow but misses declaration output or dependency order.
Recognizes config issue but lacks monorepo and CI-specific diagnosis.
Answer does not show production TypeScript build debugging.

followUps.question: What would traceResolution tell you that a normal compiler error may not?
How would you prove the fix is durable and not just a one-off cache cleanup?

en.title: Debug monorepo TypeScript build
en.displayQuestion: A monorepo TypeScript build fails only in CI with stale declarations or module resolution errors. How would you diagnose it?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate senior TypeScript build debugging.
guidance: Compare local and CI inputs.
Discuss tsconfig, declarations, project references, and resolution tracing.
commonMistakes: Treating cache deletion as root cause.
Ignoring package graph and TypeScript version skew.

vi.title: Debug build TypeScript monorepo
vi.displayQuestion: Một build TypeScript trong monorepo chỉ fail ở CI với stale declaration hoặc module resolution error. Bạn chẩn đoán thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá debug build TypeScript mức senior.
guidance: So sánh input local và CI.
Nói về tsconfig, declaration, project reference và resolution tracing.
commonMistakes: Xem việc xóa cache là root cause.
Bỏ qua package graph và lệch version TypeScript.

ja.title: monorepo TypeScript build debug

ja.displayIntent: senior TypeScript build debugging を評価する。
guidance: local と CI の input を比較する。
tsconfig、declaration、project reference、resolution tracing を話す。

intent: Assess senior understanding of declaration files, module augmentation, global augmentation, and safe typing of third-party libraries.
primaryQuestion: How would you type a third-party JavaScript library or safely augment an existing module in TypeScript?

expectedSignals.label: Distinguishes writing .d.ts declarations, ambient modules, module augmentation, and global augmentation.
Keeps declarations close to the library's real runtime behavior and examples.
Avoids broad any declarations that provide false confidence.
Plans testing through type checks, sample consumer code, and upgrade monitoring.

redFlags: Declares the whole module as any and considers the work finished.
Confuses ambient module declarations with module augmentation.
Adds global types without considering pollution or collisions.

scoringHints.description: Explains declaration authoring, augmentation boundaries, runtime fidelity, type tests, and maintenance.
Understands declaration basics but misses global pollution or upgrade risk.
Can patch a type error but not design or maintain reliable declarations.
Answer does not show declaration-file competence.

en.title: Declaration files and augmentation

en.displayIntent: Check declaration-file and augmentation expertise.
guidance: Separate ambient declarations from module augmentation.
Discuss runtime fidelity and type tests.
commonMistakes: Typing the whole library as any.
Adding broad globals that collide with other code.

vi.title: Declaration file và augmentation

vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về declaration file và augmentation.
guidance: Tách ambient declaration khỏi module augmentation.
Nói về độ khớp với runtime và type test.
commonMistakes: Type cả thư viện là any.
Thêm global quá rộng gây collision.

guidance: ambient declaration と module augmentation を分ける。
runtime fidelity と type test を話す。

intent: Evaluate senior architecture judgment around keeping TypeScript types, runtime schemas, API contracts, and generated clients consistent.
primaryQuestion: For a product with frontend and backend TypeScript, how would you keep API contracts, runtime validation, and shared types in sync?

expectedSignals.label: Compares options such as shared packages, OpenAPI/GraphQL generation, schema-first validation, or code-first schema generation.
Addresses versioning, backwards compatibility, deployment order, and consumer upgrade paths.
Keeps runtime validation at boundaries instead of relying only on shared compile-time types.
Uses CI checks, contract tests, generated clients, or schema diffing to prevent drift.

redFlags: Assumes a shared TypeScript interface alone validates API traffic.
Ignores independent deployment and version skew between frontend and backend.
Has no mechanism to detect contract drift.

scoringHints.description: Balances shared types, generated contracts, runtime validation, versioning, CI checks, and deployment sequencing.
Good sync strategy but misses runtime validation or deployment skew.
Suggests sharing interfaces but lacks end-to-end contract controls.
Answer lacks architecture-level contract reasoning.

followUps.question: What are the trade-offs between shared TypeScript types and OpenAPI-generated clients?
What CI signal would catch a breaking contract change before deployment?

Include versioning, deployment order, and validation.
commonMistakes: Relying only on shared interfaces.
Ignoring frontend/backend version skew.

Bao gồm versioning, thứ tự deploy và validation.

Bỏ qua lệch version giữa frontend/backend.

intent: Verify senior ownership of a JavaScript-to-TypeScript migration or a large TypeScript strictness improvement.
primaryQuestion: You mention migrating a codebase to TypeScript or improving TypeScript strictness. What decisions were yours, what changed technically, and how did you measure success?

expectedSignals.label: Separates personal decisions from team execution.
Describes migration scope, sequencing, tsconfig choices, typing strategy, and suppression policy.
Mentions tests, CI gates, type coverage, bug reduction, developer workflow, or build-time impacts.
Explains rollout risk, compatibility plan, and what was intentionally deferred.

redFlags: Says 'we migrated to TypeScript' without naming personal decisions.
Only counts files renamed to .ts without discussing type quality.
Cannot provide baseline, metric, or observable outcome.

scoringHints.description: Shows verifiable senior migration ownership with technical choices, rollout control, and measured outcomes.
Real migration involvement but ownership, metrics, or suppression policy is incomplete.
Likely participated but cannot demonstrate senior decision-making.
Claim cannot be validated from the answer.

followUps.question: Which TypeScript migration decision would not have happened without you?
What baseline and final signal proved the migration improved quality or velocity?

en.title: Verify TypeScript migration ownership
en.displayQuestion: You mention migrating a codebase to TypeScript or improving TypeScript strictness. What decisions were yours, what changed technically, and how did you measure success?
en.displayIntent: Verify senior ownership of TypeScript migration work.
guidance: Separate your decisions from team work.
Include technical changes, rollout risk, and outcome evidence.
commonMistakes: Using only 'we migrated' language.
Counting file renames instead of type-safety improvement.

vi.title: Xác minh ownership migration TypeScript
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn nói mình migration codebase sang TypeScript hoặc cải thiện TypeScript strictness. Quyết định nào là của bạn, kỹ thuật đã thay đổi gì, và bạn đo success thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Xác minh ownership senior trong công việc migration TypeScript.
guidance: Tách quyết định của bạn khỏi công việc của team.
Bao gồm thay đổi kỹ thuật, rủi ro rollout và bằng chứng outcome.
commonMistakes: Chỉ nói 'chúng tôi đã migrate'.
Đếm file rename thay vì cải thiện type-safety.

ja.title: TypeScript migration ownership 確認
ja.displayQuestion: codebase を TypeScript に移行した、または TypeScript strictness を改善したとあります。どの判断があなたのもので、技術的に何が変わり、success をどう測りましたか。
ja.displayIntent: TypeScript migration work の senior ownership を確認する。
guidance: 自分の判断と team work を分ける。
